entries.short: Settle the table. Lights down when you are ready to wake the first player.
entries.detail: Checklist: confirm all eyes closed; note any day deaths already marked; keep this phone private.

entries.short: Wake the Minions. Show them each other and the Demon.
entries.detail: Point silently; do not speak character names aloud if the table can hear. Put heads down when done.

entries.short: Wake the Demon. Show them their Minions, then three bluff characters.
entries.detail: Record the three bluffs below before you continue. Bluffs must be good characters not in play.

entries.short: Dawn. Everyone may open their eyes.
entries.detail: Announce any deaths you decided at night. Do not invent day nominations here — move to table talk.

entries.short: Wake {Role}. Follow the short procedure, then put them back to sleep.
entries.detail: Show a Townsfolk token and point to two players, one of whom is that Townsfolk. ST judges the answer — this app does not auto-resolve.

entries.short: Wake {Role}. Follow the short procedure, then put them back to sleep.
entries.detail: Show an Outsider token and point to two players, one of whom is that Outsider (or show that no Outsiders are in play). ST judges the answer — this app does not auto-resolve.

entries.short: Wake {Role}. Follow the short procedure, then put them back to sleep.
entries.detail: Show a Minion token and point to two players, one of whom is that Minion. ST judges the answer — this app does not auto-resolve.

entries.short: Wake {Role}. Follow the short procedure, then put them back to sleep.
entries.detail: Show a finger count for how many pairs of neighbouring evil players there are. ST judges the answer — this app does not auto-resolve.

entries.short: Wake {Role}. Follow the short procedure, then put them back to sleep.
entries.detail: Show 0, 1, or 2 for how many of their living neighbours are evil. ST judges the answer — this app does not auto-resolve.

entries.short: Wake {Role}. Follow the short procedure, then put them back to sleep.
entries.detail: They point to two players. Nod or shake for whether one is the Demon (you may treat one player as a red herring). ST judges the answer — this app does not auto-resolve.

entries.short: Wake {Role}. Follow the short procedure, then put them back to sleep.
entries.detail: If someone was executed today, show that player's character token. ST judges the answer — this app does not auto-resolve.

entries.short: Wake {Role}. Follow the short procedure, then put them back to sleep.
entries.detail: Clear the previous protect mark. They choose a player (not themself) to protect from the Demon tonight. ST judges the answer — this app does not auto-resolve.

entries.short: Wake {Role}. Follow the short procedure, then put them back to sleep.
entries.detail: If they died tonight, they point to a player — show that player's character token. ST judges the answer — this app does not auto-resolve.

entries.short: Wake {Role}. Follow the short procedure, then put them back to sleep.
entries.detail: They point to a player. Mark that player as Master. ST judges the answer — this app does not auto-resolve.

entries.short: Wake {Role}. They choose a player to poison tonight.
entries.detail: Clear any previous poison mark, then mark their choice. ST judges the answer — this app does not auto-resolve.

entries.short: Wake the Spy. Orient the grimoire carefully before you show anything.
entries.detail: Keep ST-private chrome out of sight. Tip the device or cover headers before the Spy looks. (Dedicated shutter view is deferred.)

entries.short: Wake {Role}. Check whether their conditional ability matters tonight.
entries.detail: If the Imp dies while enough players are alive, the Scarlet Woman becomes the Demon — update the Grimoire if that happened. This is a tip for you; the app does not auto-swap.

entries.short: Wake the Demon. They choose a player to kill tonight.
entries.detail: Mark deaths on the Grimoire when resolved. Star-pass: if the Imp kills themself, update who is the Demon on the Grimoire.
